subject: Statistics
title: Power Laws: The 80/20 Rule
description: One-minute skill: Power Laws: The 80/20 Rule.

hook.text: 80% of wealth belongs to 20% of people. 80% of sales come from 20% of products. This repeats everywhere.

buildup.text: Power law distributions have a few extreme values and many small ones. The tail stretches endlessly.

discovery.text: Unlike bell curves, power laws have no typical value. Rare extremes dominate the total.

twist.text: Earthquakes, bestsellers, and web traffic follow power laws. Outliers aren't anomalies — they're the rule.

climax.text: Planning for the average in a power law world is dangerous. The extremes define the system.

punchline.text: In power law worlds, the average is a lie.

quiz.question: What makes power laws different from bell curves?
options: A few extreme values dominate — there is no 'typical' value
Values always cluster around the mean
Power laws only apply to financial data
